summary: Cathy O'Neil announces the launch of Data Justice, a new collaborative project with lawyer Nathan Newman and law professor Frank Pasquale. The initiative aims to challenge the exploitation and economic inequality arising from big data practices. The project brings together expertise from law, computer science, and technology to build a movement for data justice. O'Neil positions this as a continuation of the critical work her collaborators have already done on algorithmic discrimination and corporate opacity.
key_points: Data Justice is a cross-disciplinary collaboration between a data scientist, lawyer, and law professor
The project specifically targets economic inequality and exploitation enabled by big data
The initiative aims to build a broader movement, not just conduct academic research
The team brings together complementary expertise: O'Neil's data science background, Newman's policy advocacy on Google ad discrimination, and Pasquale's legal scholarship on algorithmic opacity
money_quotes: Challenging Rising Exploitation and Economic Inequality from Big Data
We hope to build a movement on the data justice front by working across various disciplines like law, computer science, and technology
key_insight: Addressing the harms of big data requires cross-disciplinary collaboration between technologists, lawyers, and policy advocates to build a movement for accountability.
